entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The deterioration of land quality and productivity that acts as an impediment to meeting several SDGs

entities.name: LAND RESTORATION
entities.description: The process of restoring degraded land and soil to improve its quality and function

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS
entities.description: A set of global goals established by the United Nations to be achieved by 2030

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION NEUTRALITY
entities.description: An agreed-upon objective to maintain or enhance the amount and quality of land resources

entities.name: UNITED NATIONS
entities.description: The international organization that established the SDGs and aims for land degradation neutrality

entities.name: DESERTIFICATION
entities.description: The process by which fertile land becomes desert, typically as a result of drought or deforestation

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall leading to water shortage and land degradation

entities.name: FLOODS
entities.description: An overflow of water that submerges land, contributing to land degradation

entities.name: CONFERENCE OF PARTIES
entities.description: The governing body of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification

entities.name: UNITED NATIONS CONVENTION TO COMBAT DESERTIFICATION
entities.description: An international legal agreement to combat desertification and mitigate the effects of drought

entities.name: TARGET 15.3
entities.description: A specific target within the SDGs that calls to combat desertification and restore degraded land to achieve a land degradation-neutral world

relationships.description: calls to restore land affected by
calls to restore land affected by

relationships.target: UNITED NATIONS CONVENTION TO COMBAT DESERTIFICATION

relationships.source: UNITED NATIONS CONVENTION TO COMBAT DESERTIFICATION
